hero_subtitle: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs into one compliance workflow to manage document exceptions, missing files, and audit-ready verification case handling.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to manage missing or problematic documents within disconnected verification systems. This slows onboarding and weakens audit trails, making it harder to maintain clear evidence and consistent risk-based review decisions.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider results with exception workflows to create structured cases that guide teams through missing or problematic document resolution with full audit evidence and decision traceability.
workflow_title: From document issues to exception resolution
workflow_description: SFTY routes flagged documents through exception handling, risk-based review, case creation, decision logging and evidence preservation for controlled onboarding workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One audit-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects KYC and KYB document exceptions into structured workflows, cases, decisions, and audit evidence to streamline review and support clear onboarding control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with controlled workflows that preserve evidence and enable governance for document exceptions, audit readiness and scalable compliance deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support document exception handling workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how document issues route into onboarding exceptions
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence preserves onboarding document reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Compare deployment options for secure compliance document workflows
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY support KYC document exceptions?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures document exceptions into defined workflows, routing issues like missing or invalid files for review and resolution within the broader onboarding process.
faq_2_question: How are document exceptions routed and handled?
faq_2_answer: Document exceptions trigger case creation with automated routing to designated compliance reviewers, supporting timely handling and escalation as part of structured workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve onboarding evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves all onboarding decisions, document verification results and exception handling records as audit-ready evidence linked to customer profiles and cases.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing KYC providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects outputs from multiple KYC and KYB providers to connect them into a consistent compliance workflow without replacing any underlying services.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC and KYB providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above existing KYC and KYB providers, turning their outputs into structured reviews, exception workflows, evidence and reporting rather than replacing them.
final_title: Structure onboarding exceptions into controlled workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY routes document issues and missing files into traceable exception cases with audit-ready evidence and case management.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
